primary_sources.title: The May 13 Tragedy: A Report (National Operations Council, 9 October 1969)
Kua, K.S. May 13: Declassified Documents on the Malaysian Riots of 1969 (Suaram, 2007) — declassified UK FCO files

location: Kuala Lumpur (initially); spread to Selangor and other Peninsular states

estimated_participants.source_for_estimate: NOC 1969 report; declassified UK FCO files

demands: Contested. Reactive ethnic mobilisation following 10 May 1969 General Election results (Alliance loses two-thirds majority; opposition parades through KL the following days)
notes: Officially 196 killed (NOC 1969 figure); independent estimates range higher. Parliament suspended; National Operations Council (NOC) under DPM Razak Hussein governs by emergency decree until February 1971. Outcome: New Economic Policy (1971), Sedition Act amended to criminalise questioning of Article 153, constitutional amendments entrenching Malay-rulers-Islam complex. P3-to-P4 regime transition fires here. Casualty figures are the official NOC count; declassified UK FCO files suggest higher numbers, see Kua 2007.
